what_it_does: Runs a manual registry-generation convergence over ONE mockup file: grounds via run_role(screen_reader), maps register_element over candidate UI elements, reduces by clustering/dedup, then confirms each cluster representative with run_jury + a deterministic refcheck, returning {confirmed, flagged} dossiers and persisting a batch artifact JSON.
observations: Docstring frames this as the REUSABLE MANUAL form of design/_system/registry-generation.cascade.json, paralleling exocortex_ingest.py

Explicitly supplies three engine seams the cascade spec flags as not end-to-end runnable: resolve-once shared exemplars/inventory ctx, the {mockup}-keyed screen_reader ground wiring, and the confirm jury+refcheck firing
Builds an in-memory Role variant (reg_v) adding 'ground' as a bare-name shared input to register_element's input_addresses
Loads confirm_status via importlib from roles/confirm_registration.py
_grounding_payload assembles exemplars from addresses.json, feature ids from register.json, and a fixed capability vocab (pointable, spotlit, presentable, openable, driven)
register_element emits a `grounding` TAG (built|proposed|uncertain|defer) rather than a confidence float (cited as G16)
FLAG-don't-drop policy: floor-fail or non-built grounding marks for operator scrutiny, never dropped
Chain is GROUND -> MAP(run_items) -> REDUCE(run_reduce cluster mode, threshold 0.85) -> CONFIRM(per-cluster representative)
Hardcodes EXEMPLAR_KEYS for ui:// inbox/toolbar surfaces and chdir to /home/tim/company in __main__
Persists rg10-batch-<mockup>.json described as a readable batch, NOT a registry write
standouts: States the bounded manual run IS the executable spec/reference for what the engine seam must later auto-thread
Two-layer confirm combining a stochastic jury verdict with a deterministic refcheck floor
Distinction that the floor PROPOSES only — never resolve/approve/dispatch (operator-gated path comes after)
conventions: sys.path.insert + relative file opens assume cwd is the repo root
verbose progress printing with [GROUND]/[MAP]/[REDUCE]/[OK]/[FLAG] phase tags
References labelled steps (RG3-RG6, RG8, RG9, G16, V-A/RG10) tying code to an external numbering scheme
Uses runtime.cognition (C) helpers run_role/run_items/run_reduce/run_jury as the composition primitives
concerns: Many bare open() calls without context managers or explicit encoding
Truncations (mockup[:8000], outerHTML[:600], max_tokens caps) are silent and could drop content
importlib dynamic load of confirm_registration.py duplicates a module that may already be importable
notes: A self-described manual reference implementation of the registry-generation cascade that hand-threads three unbuilt engine seams to prove the convergence over a single mockup.
questions: What downstream step (RG8 PROPOSE / RG9 WRITE-BACK) consumes the persisted rg10-batch artifact?
Where is the cluster_threshold 0.85 value justified?
What defines the candidates.json schema and how is it generated?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: the docstring's stated purpose — manually supplying the three engine seams as an executable reference and producing a confirmed/flagged set — matches the code's GROUND/MAP/REDUCE/CONFIRM structure and artifact output.
apparent_themes: Grounding via real inventory/exemplars rather than free generation
FLAG-don't-drop and operator-gated write-back as safety discipline
Tags over confidence floats for grounding status
intention_signals: Trying to make an as-yet-unrunnable cascade spec concretely executable by hand-wiring its open seams, so the bounded run doubles as both a proof and the reference for what the engine should later automate.
novelty: The explicit framing of a manual script as the live specification for future engine auto-threading, plus the dedup-then-confirm-representative pattern combining jury and deterministic floor.
contribution: Contributes an executable, seam-by-seam manual realization of the registry-generation convergence (ground/map/reduce/confirm over one mockup) with a two-layer jury+refcheck confirm and FLAG-don't-drop output — the hand-threaded form of three engine seams a fused system would keep.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that manually runs the registry-generation convergence over one mockup — grounding, mapping register_element across UI candidates, clustering, and jury+refcheck confirming into confirmed/flagged dossiers.
intention_for_embedding: To make an otherwise non-runnable registry-generation cascade executable by hand-supplying its three open engine seams, serving as both convergence proof and the reference for future automation.
